items.title: India's Marine Food Export Reaches Record High of Over Rs 72,000 Crore
items.whyInNews: India's marine food export has reached a record high of over Rs 72,000 crore, with frozen shrimp being the leading export item. The United States and China are the top importers of Indian marine products.
items.summary: The export of marine food products from India has reached an all-time high of over Rs 72,000 crore. Frozen shrimp is the leading export item, with the United States and China being the top importers. This achievement is a significant milestone in the history of marine product exports from India.
keyPoints: India's marine food export has reached a record high of over Rs 72,000 crore
Frozen shrimp is the leading export item
The United States and China are the top importers of Indian marine products
This achievement is a significant milestone in the history of marine product exports from India
importantPoints: The Marine Products Export Development Authority (MPEDA) was established in 1972 and is headquartered in Kochi
The Export-Import Bank of India was established under the Export-Import Bank of India Act, 1981
India's marine food export has been increasing steadily over the years, with a growth rate of 10% in the last fiscal year
The export of marine food products is a significant contributor to India's foreign exchange earnings
The government has implemented various schemes to promote the export of marine food products, including the Marine Products Export Development Authority (MPEDA) scheme
items.examRelevance: Economy: Foreign Trade and Balance of Payments

items.title: India and South Korea Sign MoU to Strengthen Cooperation in MSME Sector
items.whyInNews: India and South Korea have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to strengthen cooperation in the Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSME) sector. The MoU aims to promote mutual cooperation and exchange of information between the two countries in the MSME sector.
items.summary: The MoU was signed between the Ministry of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises of India and the Ministry of Trade, Industry, and Energy of South Korea. The agreement aims to promote mutual cooperation and exchange of information between the two countries in the MSME sector. This cooperation is expected to enhance the competitiveness of MSMEs in both countries.
keyPoints: India and South Korea have signed an MoU to strengthen cooperation in the MSME sector
The MoU aims to promote mutual cooperation and exchange of information between the two countries
The agreement is expected to enhance the competitiveness of MSMEs in both countries
The MoU was signed between the Ministry of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises of India and the Ministry of Trade, Industry, and Energy of South Korea
importantPoints: The Ministry of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises was established in 2007 and is headquartered in New Delhi
The Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises Development Act, 2006, provides the framework for the development of MSMEs in India
South Korea is one of the largest trading partners of India, with bilateral trade valued at over $20 billion
The cooperation between India and South Korea in the MSME sector is expected to create new opportunities for entrepreneurs and businesses in both countries
The agreement is in line with the government's initiative to promote the growth of MSMEs in India

items.title: National Mission for Clean Ganga Achieves Significant Milestones in Capacity Building and Infrastructure Development
items.whyInNews: The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) has achieved significant milestones in capacity building and infrastructure development in the current financial year. The mission has completed several projects, including the construction of sewage treatment plants and the development of riverfronts.
items.summary: The NMCG has achieved significant milestones in capacity building and infrastructure development in the current financial year. The mission has completed several projects, including the construction of sewage treatment plants and the development of riverfronts. This achievement is a major step towards the conservation of the Ganga River and the improvement of the environment in the Ganga basin.

The development of riverfronts is a major component of the NMCG's infrastructure development plan

importantPoints: The National Mission for Clean Ganga was established in 2011 and is headquartered in New Delhi

The mission has a budget of over Rs 20,000 crore for the current financial year
The NMCG is working in partnership with several state governments and other stakeholders to achieve its objectives

items.title: India and Bhutan Hold 7th Joint Group on Customs Meeting in Kerala
items.whyInNews: India and Bhutan have held the 7th Joint Group on Customs meeting in Kerala. The meeting discussed various issues related to customs and trade between the two countries.
items.summary: The 7th Joint Group on Customs meeting between India and Bhutan was held in Kerala. The meeting discussed various issues related to customs and trade between the two countries, including the simplification of customs procedures and the promotion of trade facilitation. This meeting is a significant step towards strengthening the bilateral trade relationship between India and Bhutan.
keyPoints: India and Bhutan have held the 7th Joint Group on Customs meeting in Kerala
The meeting discussed various issues related to customs and trade between the two countries
The simplification of customs procedures and the promotion of trade facilitation were major agenda items

importantPoints: The Joint Group on Customs was established in 2003 to promote cooperation in customs and trade between India and Bhutan
The India-Bhutan Trade Agreement was signed in 1974 and has been amended several times since then
Bhutan is one of the largest trading partners of India in the South Asian region
The bilateral trade between India and Bhutan is valued at over Rs 10,000 crore
The meeting is in line with the government's initiative to promote trade and economic cooperation with neighboring countries

items.title: Vice President Radhakrishnan Calls for More Women in Civil Services

items.title: India to Never Bow to Terror: PM Modi on Pahalgam Attack Anniversary
items.whyInNews: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated that India will never bow to any form of terror on the anniversary of the Pahalgam attack. The attack was carried out by the Pakistan-based terror group Lashkar-e-Taiba on April 22, 2025, killing 26 innocent people.
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has reiterated India's strong stance against terrorism, stating that the country will never bow to any form of terror. This statement comes on the anniversary of the Pahalgam attack, which occurred on April 22, 2025, in Jammu and Kashmir. The attack resulted in the loss of 26 innocent lives, with the victims being mostly tourists from across India.
keyPoints: Prime Minister Narendra Modi made the statement on April 22, 2026.
The Pahalgam attack occurred on April 22, 2025, in Jammu and Kashmir.
The attack was carried out by the Pakistan-based terror group Lashkar-e-Taiba.
26 innocent people lost their lives in the attack.
importantPoints: Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) is a militant Islamist organization founded in 1990 and headquartered in Muridke, Pakistan.
Article 355 of the Indian Constitution pertains to the duty of the Union to protect states against external aggression and internal disturbance.
The Pahalgam attack is one of the many instances of cross-border terrorism faced by India.
India has been actively engaged in global efforts to combat terrorism, including participation in the United Nations Counter-Terrorism Committee.
The statement by PM Modi underscores India's commitment to fighting terrorism and ensuring national security.
items.examRelevance: GK: International Relations, Polity: Constitutional Provisions

items.title: IPL 2026: Abhishek and Malinga Star in SRH's Win Over DC
items.whyInNews: Abhishek scored an unbeaten 135, while Malinga took four wickets as Sunrisers Hyderabad defeated Delhi Capitals in the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026.

items.title: Supreme Court to Hear Plea Challenging National Investigation Agency Act, 2008
items.whyInNews: The Supreme Court has agreed to hear a plea challenging the validity of the National Investigation Agency (NIA) Act, 2008. The plea claims that the Act is violative of Article 14 of the Constitution and beyond the legislative competence of the Centre.
items.summary: The Supreme Court of India has decided to hear a plea that challenges the validity of the National Investigation Agency (NIA) Act, 2008. The plea argues that the Act violates Article 14 of the Indian Constitution, which guarantees equality before the law, and is also beyond the legislative competence of the Central government. This decision is significant as it may have implications for the functioning of the NIA and the investigation of terror cases in India.
keyPoints: The Supreme Court will hear a plea challenging the NIA Act, 2008.
The plea claims that the Act violates Article 14 of the Constitution.
The Act is also challenged for being beyond the legislative competence of the Centre.
The NIA was established in 2008 to investigate and prosecute terror cases.
importantPoints: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) was established in 2008 and is headquartered in New Delhi, India.
Article 14 of the Indian Constitution pertains to equality before the law.
The NIA Act, 2008, gives the agency the power to investigate and prosecute terror cases across India.
The plea challenging the NIA Act, 2008, may have significant implications for the investigation of terror cases in India.
The Supreme Court's decision will be crucial in determining the validity and scope of the NIA Act, 2008.
items.examRelevance: Polity: Constitutional Provisions, GK: National Investigation Agency
